author: Miriam Polzer
message_subject: Ed25519: Point-based separation logic for doubling
message_body:
Convert the doubling function to a point based format, the rest of the
functions will follow.

Add a tactic for deallocation, to be used in other proofs too.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This flag results in running symex on the asm side first, resulting in
easier debugging when there is an error in symbolically executing the
assembly at all.

author: Andres Erbsen
message_subject: update Weierstrass Affine, Jacobian with Proper, add laws

author: Samuel Gruetter
message_subject: update to a rupicola/bedrock2 where wp is complete wrt exec

author: Dustin Jamner
message_subject: Investigate generating better low-level code by bringing derivation closer to the spec

author: Dustin Jamner
message_subject: Get Qed to go through for chacha20 derivation

author: Samuel Tian
message_subject: fix duplicated multiplication, need to push reduce3 through bounds inference

author: Samuel Tian
message_subject: implement reduce3, intended to be cmovz

author: Samuel Tian
message_subject: add list of identifiers to cache reify

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add `--debug` flag and debug info for rewriting

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add some operations to equivalence checker (#1191)
message_body: We now support `mul`, `push`, `pop`.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This allows us to check multiple assembly files without having to
resynthesize the PHOAS input, as suggested in
https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/issues/1170#issuecomment-1076886198.

What remains is to update the Makefile generation to take advantage of
this.

author: Jason Gross
message_body: So that most of it happens inside the symex monad

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Spec out the biggest blocks of the equivalence checker
message_body: Pair-coded with Andres

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
We want to return the assembly functions not matched by anything
requested, for the error messages.  We were missing a negation in
filtering before.  Oops.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Closes #791

This should resolve the question of endianess of encoding.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add some debugging of bedrock2 output
message_body: I leave it in this file so that if I need to debug more in the future, I have a reference to start from

author: Jason Gross
message_body: Now it automatically removes reflexive versions of things

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Some proofs were changed because I originally switched `Qed` to
`Defined`, and changed proofs to fit that, and didn't bother changing
the proofs back when I decided `Defined` wasn't needed.

The primary thing this commit adds is an improvement to the
`reflect_beq_to_eq` tactic.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Currently we don't handle rules that require "concrete list of cons
cells" nor rules that require "concrete nat literal to do recursion on";
both of these require special treatment, to be implemented in an
upcoming commit.

The reifier is kind-of slow, alas.

author: Jade Philipoom
message_subject: preliminary version of Montgomery reduce in new pipeline; includes adding support for Z.leb and several saturated-arith operations (add_get_carry, add_with_get_carry, sub_get_borrow, mul_split, zselect, and add_modulo)

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: [demo] Add a list rec canonicalization pass

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
* [demo] Add reification in src/Experiments/SimplyTypedArithmetic.v

It's rather verbose, unfortunately.  The reification also doesn't have
any of the nice debugging features of the version of reification in
Compilers, because that's even more boilerplate.  Not sure if I should
add that back in, at the moment.

Also, for some strange reason, places where `constr`s fail to typecheck
seem to induce backtracking where I don't think they should, and I'm not
sure what's going on...

* [demo] Add more namespacing

* Update llet notation, update is_known_const name

As per code review suggestions

* Namespace var_context, add some coqbug references

* Rename is_type_arg to is_template_parameter

As per https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/pull/275#discussion_r153036059

* Simplify the logic around delayed arguments a bit

We no longer pass around dummy markers in the tuple of arguments.

* [demo] More informative reification error messages

This time without exponential slowdown in failure cases and without
needing to manually think up all of the possible errors and write them
out.

Possible thanks to Hugo's comment at
https://github.com/coq/coq/issues/6252#issuecomment-347041995

* [demo] respond to code review, add comments

* Update documentation with suggestions from Andres

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This brings in most of the changes that I made when figuring out how to
integrate montgomery into the framework.

The code is a bit slower because the we drop `Print Assumptions` at the
bottom of each synthesis problem, to record that things are closed under
the global context.  If we remove this, we get back the time that we
lost with this commit.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Now there's a version that handles things in Saturated.Core, and in
Wrappers.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Factor out some bits of ladderstep preglue
message_body:
This requires some changes to nonzero, which now needs to unfold
proj1_sig itself.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Factor out some of the preglue synthesis code
message_body:
This makes it a bit more uniform, and hopefully more automatable and
packageable.  Unfortunately, there's still no spec for this part of the
pipeline, so the tactics simply aggregate common patterns.

Alas, this also makes things a bit slower; I suspect that [Defined] is
the place where things are slower.

author: Jason Gross
message_body: This way, files importing Core don't have to keep track of the list of runtime operations, for unfoling.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Now it handles matches under binders, e.g., under dlet.  This fixes
https://github.com/mit-plv/fiat-crypto/issues/195#issuecomment-308724129.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Add reflective machinery for adc, zselect

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
Now we have a tactic [reify_context_variables] that can preprocess a
context to allow [Reify] to handle any context variables whose types are
of the form [interp_type _ _].

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: An approximately first stab DeBruijn word-size-sel

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
This is https://coq.inria.fr/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=5434, [vm_compute]
breaks an invariant asserted on line 115 of
pretyping/constr_matching.ml.  This was triggering whenever we tried to
reify one of the operations.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
We don't have working word code yet, because the reification code currently
does spurious word->N->Z->N->word conversions everywhere.  So we use Z instead.

author: Jason Gross
message_body:
By being careful about building the expressions in the first place, we
no longer need it, and can rely on dead code elimination.

author: Jason Gross
message_subject: Don't inline everything in Montgomery and Barrett
message_body:
We still use CSE in fancy machine, because we want to lift the ldi's
above the rest of the code.  However, on a quick inspection, the
algorithm no longer needs CSE to be duplicate-free.
